name: The Bicycle
tagline: Human-powered rotary drive that converts leg endurance into efficient personal transport on prepared surfaces
problem:
Walking scales poorly with distance for time-sensitive tasks; horses cost feed and care. Mid-industrial societies needed a personal vehicle that uses human food calories directly without animal overhead. Early velocipedes were heavy and bone-shaking; the safety bicycle with similar-size wheels and chain drive solved stability and gearing, enabling mass adoption.

The bicycle problem is lightweight structure plus reliable rolling bearings plus a drivetrain that tolerates grit. It couples ergonomics (crank length, saddle height) with materials (thin-walled steel tubing) and manufacturing precision (interchangeable threads).

Bicycles also democratised mobility for working classes and reshaped women's clothing and urban geography — social effects as large as engineering ones.
overview:
A modern bicycle couples a diamond or compact frame for triangular rigidity with two wheels on axles containing ball bearings. The crankset drives a chainring; a roller chain transmits torque to a rear sprocket on a hub that may include a freewheel allowing coasting. Steering uses a fork with rake and trail geometry that provides self-centering tendency at speed.

Brakes evolved from spoon brakes on tyres to calliper rim brakes and disc brakes; each trades complexity for wet-weather performance. Tyres are pneumatic for low rolling resistance and bump absorption; they require airtight rims and valves.

Gearing uses derailleurs or internal hubs to match human cadence to terrain. Tensioners and limit screws prevent chain drop; alignment of hanger faces matters to sub-millimetre scales.

Manufacturing depends on steel tubing, lugged or welded joints, and heat-treated cranks — the bicycle is an early triumph of thin-walled metal and precision threads.

principles.name: Rolling resistance versus aero drag
principles.explanation: At low speeds, rolling losses dominate; at high speeds, aerodynamic drag dominates — shaping rider position matters more than marginal bearing gains at racing speeds.

principles.name: Bicycle self-stability
principles.explanation: Trail, gyroscopic effects, and mass distribution interact; riders actively steer, but geometric trail provides a restoring torque tendency.

principles.name: Chain articulation fatigue
principles.explanation: Roller chains fail by pin wear and plate fatigue; cleanliness and lubrication dominate life more than steel grade once above baseline.

principles.name: Stress concentration at welds
principles.explanation: Heat-affected zones change grain structure; proper penetration and post-weld alignment prevent crack initiation at joints.

components.name: Frame
components.function: Carries rider loads and connects all subsystems
components.madeFrom: Steel, aluminium, carbon fibre in modern eras
components.criticalNote: Check for cracks at welds after hard impacts

components.name: Fork and headset
components.function: Steers front wheel with defined rake/trail
components.madeFrom: Steel or aluminium legs, steerer tube
components.criticalNote: Indexed bearings must be preloaded correctly — too loose wobbles, too tight binds

components.name: Wheels
components.function: Rolling support with tensioned spokes distributing loads
components.madeFrom: Rim, spokes, nipples, hub, tyre, tube or tubeless seal
components.criticalNote: Uneven spoke tension creates lateral hops under load

components.name: Drivetrain
components.function: Converts leg torque to rear wheel rotation
components.position: Chain path from chainring to cassette
components.madeFrom: Steel chain, hardened teeth, derailleur linkage
components.criticalNote: Cross-chaining accelerates wear — avoid extreme combos

components.name: Brakes
components.madeFrom: Aluminium rims, rubber pads, steel cables, hydraulic fluid later
components.criticalNote: Wet rims reduce friction — test before descending

rawMaterials.name: Steel tubing
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Aluminium from electrolysis once power exists
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Butted drawing, mitering, welding or brazing

rawMaterials.name: Rubber (natural or synthetic)
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Silicone or polyurethane variants in advanced labs

rawMaterials.name: Lubricants
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: PFPE greases for vacuum-adjacent weird environments
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Clean before relube — solvent risks to seals

buildSteps.title: Miter and tack-weld frame
buildSteps.description: Cut tubes to angles from jig; tack in sequence to control distortion; verify head tube and bottom bracket alignment on surface plate if possible.
prerequisiteTools: TIG welder or oxy torch for brazing
buildSteps.warningNote: Welding fumes — ventilation; UV eye damage

buildSteps.title: Face and chase interfaces
buildSteps.description: Machine head tube faces and bottom bracket threads square to axis; ream seat tube for correct seat post fit.
buildSteps.warningNote: Skewed BB shell ruins chainline permanently

buildSteps.title: Build wheels
buildSteps.description: Lace spokes in pattern; tension evenly to target deflection; dish rim centred to hub; stress-relieve by squeezing pairs.
buildSteps.warningNote: Undertensioned spokes fatigue and break in clusters

buildSteps.title: Assemble drivetrain and align derailleur
buildSteps.description: Set hanger parallel; limit screws to prevent overshift; set B-gap per manufacturer; index shifter clicks.
buildSteps.warningNote: Front derailleur can throw chain into spokes — set limits conservatively

buildSteps.title: Safety check and test ride
buildSteps.description: Verify torque on stem, bars, seat post, thru-axles; brake hard walk test; rolling jump test off curb at low speed listening for creaks.
buildSteps.warningNote: Always wear helmet on public roads — modern injury data is unambiguous

history.event: Karl von Drais's Laufmaschine demonstrates steerable two-wheeled balance machines without pedals.
Rover safety bicycle with chain drive and similar wheels popularises the modern bicycle layout.
Mass production drops prices; bicycles become everyday transport for millions before automobile primacy in some regions.

inventors.name: John Kemp Starley
inventors.contribution: Rover safety bicycle — chain drive and stable wheel sizing

impact:
Bicycles improved urban worker mobility, enabled rural postal routes, and became sport infrastructure. They previewed mass personal mechanised transport and still outperform many vehicles on energy per kilometre.

In recovery scenarios, bicycles are rebuildable with a lathe, welder, and wire wheel skills — a mid-tier industrial product, not stone-age.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Bicycle
sources.note: Mechanics, history, and safety culture references

sources.title: OSHA
sources.note: Workshop safety for fabrication and repair of cycle components
